{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-8","id":"msg_01BrQBQf3jccXW1x9FjjJv6v","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01LSSpYWz95277BkYjVfApqG","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"cd /app\npython3 - <<'PY'\nimport csv\nfrom collections import defaultdict\n\n# Load products thresholds\nprod = {}\nwith open('products.csv') as f:\n    for r in csv.DictReader(f):\n        prod[r['product_id']] = float(r['quality_rejection_threshold_pct'])\n\n# Check rejection rule\nrej_above=0; rej_below=0; acc_above=0; acc_below=0\nwith open('quality_inspections.csv') as f:\n    for r in csv.DictReader(f):\n        p = r['product_id']; pct=float(r['rejected_pct']); res=r['inspection_result']\n        thr = prod[p]\n        if res=='REJECTED':\n            if pct>thr: rej_above+=1\n            else: rej_below+=1\n        else:\n            if pct>thr: acc_above+=1\n            else: acc_below+=1\nprint(\"REJECTED above thr:\", rej_above, \" REJECTED below thr:\", rej_below)\nprint(\"ACCEPTED above thr:\", acc_above, \" ACCEPTED below thr:\", acc_below)\nPY","description":"Verify rejection threshold logic"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":424,"cache_read_input_tokens":27028,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":424,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":42,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"78d2457f-49d8-488b-9183-d64919fdddae","uuid":"3309fd38-38f3-4c71-b883-db4a12eab98c","request_id":"req_011CbfBWonSmdjTFeUpVfwM6"}

/app\npython3 - <<'PY'\nimport os, re, csv, glob\n\n# Parse all service reports\nrows=[]\nfor path in glob.glob('raw_service_reports/day_*/*.txt'):\n    d={}\n    with open(path) as f:\n        for {"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-8","id":"msg_01RNVh4ekXhY2VcfB95PJFLP","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01NrLsx2ALyFdxZ9s5A4fcYT","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"cd /app\npython3 - <<'PY'\nimport csv\nfrom collections import defaultdict\n\n# production_batches\nbatches={}\nwith open('production_batches.csv') as f:\n    for r in csv.DictReader(f):\n        batches[r['batch_id']]=r\nprint(\"batches:\", len(batches))\n\n# machine_assignment_log keyed by job_id\nmal={}\ndup=0\nwith open('machine_assignment_log.csv') as f:\n    for r in csv.DictReader(f):\n        if r['job_id'] in mal: dup+=1\n        mal[r['job_id']]=r\nprint(\"mal entries:\", len(mal), \"dup job_ids:\", dup)\n\n# Check every batch's job_id present in mal and machine matches\nmiss=0; mmach=0\nfor b,r in batches.items():\n    j=r['job_id']\n    if j not in mal: miss+=1; continue\n    if mal[j]['machine_id']!=r['machine_id']: mmach+=1\nprint(\"batches missing mal:\", miss, \" machine mismatch:\", mmach)\n\n# 1 batch per job? \njobcount=defaultdict(int)\nfor b,r in batches.items(): jobcount[r['job_id']]+=1\nmulti=sum(1 for j,c in jobcount.items() if c>1)\nprint(\"jobs with >1 batch:\", multi, \" total jobs in batches:\", len(jobcount))\n\n# inspection delay\nqi={}\nwith open('quality_inspections.csv') as f:\n    for r in csv.DictReader(f):\n        qi[r['batch_id']]=r\nprint(\"inspections:\", len(qi))\n# every batch inspected?\nprint(\"batches w/o inspection:\", sum(1 for b in batches if b not in qi))\nprint(\"inspections w/o batch:\", sum(1 for b in qi if b not in batches))\n\n# delay distribution\ndelays=[]\nfor b,r in qi.items():\n    pd=int(batches[b]['production_day']); idd=int(r['inspection_day'])\n    delays.append(idd-pd)\nimport statistics\nprint(\"delay min/max/mean:\", min(delays), max(delays), round(statistics.mean(delays),2))\nneg=sum(1 for d in delays if d<0)\nprint(\"negative delays:\", neg)\nPY","description":"Validate batch-job-inspection joins and delays"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":823,"cache_read_input_tokens":31183,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":823,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":41,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"78d2457f-49d8-488b-9183-d64919fdddae","uuid":"b8993343-6c3c-45a0-a7a8-b063a149c8ac","request_id":"req_011CbfBavSA98nY9oNvSkDjU"}

Now let me build the attribution pipeline and explore the rejection patterns to find the signal distinguishing faults from natural variation."}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1094,"cache_read_input_tokens":32006,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":1094,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":48,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"78d2457f-49d8-488b-9183-d64919fdddae","uuid":"7eebb61d-ecc3-45b1-80ea-9cf947a39786","request_id":"req_011CbfBbjWcb95vxrcWxdb8k"}
{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-8","id":"msg_016hhvsKtQapZcFreYnuQg2N","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01ThgVxHvDsZXEt2Tp8Wtsao","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"cd /app\npython3 - <<'PY'\nimport csv, glob\nfrom collections import defaultdict\n\ndef to_min(day, hhmm):\n    h,m=hhmm.split(':')\n    return day*1440+int(h)*60+int(m)\n\n# Maintenance events\nevents=[]\nfor path in glob.glob('raw_service_reports/day_*/*.txt'):\n    d={}\n    for line in open(path):\n        line=line.strip()\n        if ':' in line:\n            k,v=line.split(':',1); d[k.strip()]=v.strip()\n    events.append(dict(day=int(d['DAY']), wo=d['WORK ORDER'], machine=d['MACHINE'],\n        tech=d['TECHNICIAN'], wtype=d['WORK TYPE'], reason=d['REPORTED REASON'],\n        started=d['STARTED'], completed=d['COMPLETED'],\n        start_min=to_min(int(d['DAY']),d['STARTED']),\n        comp_min=to_min(int(d['DAY']),d['COMPLETED'])))\n\n# group events per machine sorted by completion\nby_machine=defaultdict(list)\nfor e in events: by_machine[e['machine']].append(e)\nfor m in by_machine: by_machine[m].sort(key=lambda e:(e['comp_min'], e['start_min']))\n\n# completed before started? check\nweird=sum(1 for e in events if e['comp_min']<e['start_min'])\nprint(\"events completed<started:\", weird)\n\n# batches + mal start times\nmal={}\nfor r in csv.DictReader(open('machine_assignment_log.csv')):\n    mal[r['job_id']]=r\nbatches={}\nfor r in csv.DictReader(open('production_batches.csv')):\n    b=r['batch_id']; j=r['job_id']\n    st=mal[j]['start_time']\n    batches[b]=dict(day=int(r['production_day']), job=j, machine=r['machine_id'],\n        product=r['product_id'], qty=int(r['produced_quantity']),\n        start_min=to_min(int(r['production_day']), st))\n\n# inspections\nqi={}\nfor r in csv.DictReader(open('quality_inspections.csv')):\n    qi[r['batch_id']]=r\n\n# Attribute each batch to a maintenance interval\n# interval of event e: (e.comp_min, next_event.start_min)\ndef attribute(b):\n    info=batches[b]\n    m=info['machine']; evs=by_machine.get(m,[])\n    # find event with largest comp_min <= start? must be strictly after completed\n    chosen=None\n    for i,e in enumerate(evs):\n        if info['start_min']>e['comp_min']:\n            # check before next event start\n            nxt=evs[i+1] if i+1<len(evs) else None\n            if nxt is None or info['start_min']<nxt['start_min']:\n                chosen=e\n                # but there could be a later event that also satisfies; we want the most recent. Keep iterating.\n    return chosen\n\n# Stats: of all batches, how many attributable\nattr_count=0; unattr=0\nfor b in batches:\n    if attribute(b): attr_count+=1\n    else: unattr+=1\nprint(\"attributable batches:\", attr_count, \"unattributable:\", unattr)\n\n# Rejected batches\nrejected=[b for b in batches if qi[b]['inspection_result']=='REJECTED']\nprint(\"rejected batches:\", len(rejected))\nra=sum(1 for b in rejected if attribute(b))\nprint(\"rejected attributable:\", ra, \"unattributable:\", len(rejected)-ra)\nPY","description":"Build attribution pipeline, check coverage"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1094,"cache_read_input_tokens":32006,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":1094,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":48,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"78d2457f-49d8-488b-9183-d64919fdddae","uuid":"e7ef2719-ac24-4e3e-9883-d57276fa3bcf","request_id":"req_011CbfBbjWcb95vxrcWxdb8k"}

batches=load_batches(); qi=load_inspections(); prod=load_products()\nby_machine=machine_intervals(events)\n\nacc=defaultdict(list)\nfor b,info in batches.items():\n    if qi[b]['result']=='ACCEPTED': acc[info['product']].append(qi[b]['rejected_pct'])\nam={p:np.mean(v) for p,v in acc.items()}; asd={p:np.std(v) for p,v in acc.items()}\n\nbatch_wo={}\nfor b,info in batches.items():\n    e=attribute(info,by_machine); batch_wo[b]=e['wo'] if e else None\nwo_event={e['wo']:e for e in events}\n\ncell=defaultdict(list)\nfor b,info in batches.items():\n    if batch_wo[b]: cell[(batch_wo[b],info['product'])].append(b)\n\nrows=[]\nfor (wo,p),bs in cell.items():\n    nrej=sum(1 for b in bs if qi[b]['result']=='REJECTED')\n    if nrej==0: continue\n    cm=np.mean([qi[b]['rejected_pct'] for b in bs])\n    cmz=(cm-am[p])/asd[p]\n    maxz=max((qi[b]['rejected_pct']-am[p])/asd[p] for b in bs)\n    rows.append((cmz, wo,p,len(bs),nrej,maxz))\nrows.sort()\n# find gap in cmz\ncz=np.array([r[0] for r in rows])\ngaps=sorted([(cz[i+1]-cz[i],cz[i],cz[i+1]) for i in range(len(cz)-1)],reverse=True)\nprint(\"Largest gaps in cell_mean_z:\")\nfor g in gaps[:8]: print(f\"  gap {g[0]:.2f} between {g[1]:.2f} and {g[2]:.2f}\")\n\nprint(f\"\\n{'cmz':>6} {'wo':>10} {'prod':>6} {'n':>3} {'rej':>3} {'rate':>5} {'maxz':>5}\")\nfor r in rows:\n    flag = \" <== FULLY REJ\" if r[4]==r[3] else \"\"\n    print(f\"{r[0]:6.2f} {r[1]:>10} {r[2]:>6} {r[3]:3d} {r[4]:3d} {r[4]/r[3]:5.2f} {r[5]:5.1f}{flag}\")\nPY","description":"Cell-mean z-scores with gap detection and rejection rates"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":7320,"cache_read_input_tokens":93910,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":7320,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":36,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"78d2457f-49d8-488b-9183-d64919fdddae","uuid":"84595379-3f5f-4602-9922-7e156329cfca","request_id":"req_011CbfCC1itCiGuy9Aba1QKu"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-8","id":"msg_01SxgXrduTfbcGGeXTySZcGf","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The cell-mean-z confounds magnitude with cell size. The real per-batch signal is `maxz` (SD above the product's natural mean). Clear faults are ≥7 SD (huge gap to 30); the natural tail tops out ~4.5 SD; there's a fuzzy zone (4.5–7). {"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-8","id":"msg_01SxgXrduTfbcGGeXTySZcGf","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01AS4YMpW41pwoxngXq6iTEz","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/app/pipeline.py","content":"\"\"\"Full attribution pipeline with configurable fault classification.\"\"\"\nfrom lib import *\nfrom collections import defaultdict\nimport numpy as np\n\nevents = load_events(); batches = load_batches(); qi = load_inspections(); prod = load_products()\nby_machine = machine_intervals(events)\nwo_event = {e['wo']: e for e in events}\n\n# natural anchor per product from accepted batches\nacc = defaultdict(list)\nfor b, info in batches.items():\n    if qi[b]['result'] == 'ACCEPTED':\n        acc[info['product']].append(qi[b]['rejected_pct'])\nam = {p: float(np.mean(v)) for p, v in acc.items()}\nasd = {p: float(np.std(v)) for p, v in acc.items()}\n\n# attribute every batch to its interval (wo)\nbatch_wo = {}\nfor b, info in batches.items():\n    e = attribute(info, by_machine)\n    batch_wo[b] = e['wo'] if e else None\n\n# build cells: (wo, product) -> list of batch_ids\ncell = defaultdict(list)\nfor b, info in batches.items():\n    if batch_wo[b]:\n        cell[(batch_wo[b], info['product'])].append(b)\n\ndef zof(b):\n    info = batches[b]; p = info['product']\n    return (qi[b]['rejected_pct'] - am[p]) / asd[p]\n\ndef cell_stats(key):\n    bs = cell[key]\n    n = len(bs)\n    rej = [b for b in bs if qi[b]['result'] == 'REJECTED']\n    maxz = max((zof(b) for b in bs), default=0.0)\n    return n, len(rej), maxz, rej\n\ndef fault_cells(z_single=7.0, z_multi=5.0):\n    \"\"\"Return set of (wo,product) cells judged faulty.\n    A cell is faulty if it has >=1 rejection AND either:\n      - it is fully rejected with n>=2 and maxz>=z_multi (rate+magnitude), or\n      - it has >=2 rejections and maxz>=z_multi, or\n      - its max batch z >= z_single (a single extreme batch).\"\"\"\n    out = {}\n    for key in cell:\n        n, k, maxz, rej = cell_stats(key)\n        if k == 0:\n            continue\n        faulty = False\n        if k >= 2 and maxz >= z_multi:\n            faulty = True\n        elif n >= 2 and k == n and maxz >= z_multi:\n            faulty = True\n        elif maxz >= z_single:\n            faulty = True\n        if faulty:\n            out[key] = (n, k, maxz, rej)\n    return out\n\ndef confirm(fcells, min_batches=3, min_wos=2):\n    \"\"\"Aggregate fault-rejected batches by technician; confirm and report products.\"\"\"\n    tech_batches = defaultdict(set)   # tech -> set of rejected batch_ids\n    tech_wos = defaultdict(set)       # tech -> set of wos\n    tech_prod_batches = defaultdict(lambda: defaultdict(set))  # tech -> product -> batches\n    for (wo, p), (n, k, maxz, rej) in fcells.items():\n        tech = wo_event[wo]['tech']\n        for b in rej:\n            tech_batches[tech].add(b)\n            tech_wos[tech].add(wo)\n            tech_prod_batches[tech][p].add(b)\n    confirmed = {}\n    for tech, bs in tech_batches.items():\n        if len(bs) >= min_batches and len(tech_wos[tech]) >= min_wos:\n            confirmed[tech] = {p: len(v) for p, v in tech_prod_batches[tech].items()}\n    return confirmed, tech_batches, tech_wos\n\ndef product_answer(confirmed):\n    prod_techs = defaultdict(set)\n    for tech, prods in confirmed.items():\n        for p in prods:\n            prod_techs[p].add(tech)\n    return {p: sorted(prod_techs[p]) for p in sorted(prod_techs)}\n\nif __name__ == '__main__':\n    import json, sys\n    fc = fault_cells()\n    confirmed, tb, tw = confirm(fc)\n    print(\"Confirmed technicians:\", len(confirmed))\n    for t in sorted(confirmed):\n        print(f\"  {t}: batches={len(tb[t])} wos={len(tw[t])} products={confirmed[t]}\")\n    ans = product_answer(confirmed)\n    print(\"\\nProduct answer:\")\n    print(json.dumps(ans, indent=0))\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":131,"cache_creation_input_tokens":13564,"cache_read_input_tokens":101230,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":13564,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":39,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"78d2457f-49d8-488b-9183-d64919fdddae","uuid":"95b6437b-ab16-4989-bbb0-c8c4da921ee2","request_id":"req_011CbfCHRoN3ozdoyTE7pjrP"}

batches = load_batches(); qi = load_inspections(); prod = load_products()\nby_machine = machine_intervals(events)\nwo_event = {e['wo']: e for e in events}\n\n# natural anchor per product from accepted batches\nacc = defaultdict(list)\nfor b, info in batches.items():\n    if qi[b]['result'] == 'ACCEPTED':\n        acc[info['product']].append(qi[b]['rejected_pct'])\nam = {p: float(np.mean(v)) for p, v in acc.items()}\nasd = {p: float(np.std(v)) for p, v in acc.items()}\n\n# attribute every batch to its interval (wo)\nbatch_wo = {}\nfor b, info in batches.items():\n    e = attribute(info, by_machine)\n    batch_wo[b] = e['wo'] if e else None\n\n# build cells: (wo, product) -> list of batch_ids\ncell = defaultdict(list)\nfor b, info in batches.items():\n    if batch_wo[b]:\n        cell[(batch_wo[b], info['product'])].append(b)\n\ndef zof(b):\n    info = batches[b]; p = info['product']\n    return (qi[b]['rejected_pct'] - am[p]) / asd[p]\n\ndef cell_stats(key):\n    bs = cell[key]\n    n = len(bs)\n    rej = [b for b in bs if qi[b]['result'] == 'REJECTED']\n    maxz = max((zof(b) for b in bs), default=0.0)\n    return n, len(rej), maxz, rej\n\ndef fault_cells(z_single=7.0, z_multi=5.0):\n    \"\"\"Return set of (wo,product) cells judged faulty.\n    A cell is faulty if it has >=1 rejection AND either:\n      - it is fully rejected with n>=2 and maxz>=z_multi (rate+magnitude), or\n      - it has >=2 rejections and maxz>=z_multi, or\n      - its max batch z >= z_single (a single extreme batch).\"\"\"\n    out = {}\n    for key in cell:\n        n, k, maxz, rej = cell_stats(key)\n        if k == 0:\n            continue\n        faulty = False\n        if k >= 2 and maxz >= z_multi:\n            faulty = True\n        elif n >= 2 and k == n and maxz >= z_multi:\n            faulty = True\n        elif maxz >= z_single:\n            faulty = True\n        if faulty:\n            out[key] = (n, k, maxz, rej)\n    return out\n\ndef confirm(fcells, min_batches=3, min_wos=2):\n    \"\"\"Aggregate fault-rejected batches by technician; confirm and report products.\"\"\"\n    tech_batches = defaultdict(set)   # tech -> set of rejected batch_ids\n    tech_wos = defaultdict(set)       # tech -> set of wos\n    tech_prod_batches = defaultdict(lambda: defaultdict(set))  # tech -> product -> batches\n    for (wo, p), (n, k, maxz, rej) in fcells.items():\n        tech = wo_event[wo]['tech']\n        for b in rej:\n            tech_batches[tech].add(b)\n            tech_wos[tech].add(wo)\n            tech_prod_batches[tech][p].add(b)\n    confirmed = {}\n    for tech, bs in tech_batches.items():\n        if len(bs) >= min_batches and len(tech_wos[tech]) >= min_wos:\n            confirmed[tech] = {p: len(v) for p, v in tech_prod_batches[tech].items()}\n    return confirmed, tech_batches, tech_wos\n\ndef product_answer(confirmed):\n    prod_techs = defaultdict(set)\n    for tech, prods in confirmed.items():\n        for p in prods:\n            prod_techs[p].add(tech)\n    return {p: sorted(prod_techs[p]) for p in sorted(prod_techs)}\n\nif __name__ == '__main__':\n    import json, sys\n    fc = fault_cells()\n    confirmed, tb, tw = confirm(fc)\n    print(\"Confirmed technicians:\", len(confirmed))\n    for t in sorted(confirmed):\n        print(f\"  {t}: batches={len(tb[t])} wos={len(tw[t])} products={confirmed[t]}\")\n    ans = product_answer(confirmed)\n    print(\"\\nProduct answer:\")\n    print(json.dumps(ans, indent=0))\n","structuredPatch":[],"originalFile":null,"userModified":false}}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-8","id":"msg_01D2XSgS6qSQ4UcgRTetv8Zz","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_016EbBnR2rD4qwHXzQgkBwBz","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"cd /app\npython3 - <<'PY'\nfrom pipeline import *\nimport numpy as np, json\n\n# ---------- Independent detector A: per-product gap-based fault cutoff ----------\n# For each product, find natural ceiling = largest rejected value still contiguous with\n# the natural tail; faults = clearly separated cluster above. Use accepted distribution\n# to set a conservative natural ceiling, then a batch is a fault if its rejected_pct\n# exceeds natural ceiling by a clear margin.\nacc=defaultdict(list); rejv=defaultdict(list)\nfor b,info in batches.items():\n    (rejv if qi[b]['result']=='REJECTED' else acc)[info['product']].append((qi[b]['rejected_pct'],b))\n# natural ceiling per product: max over (accepted max, and the natural-tail of rejections)\n# We define fault batches as those with rejected_pct >= 1.5 * threshold OR >= acc_max+0.025 (abs gap)\nfault_batches_A=set()\nfor p in rejv:\n    thr=prod[p]['threshold']; accmax=max(v for v,_ in acc[p]) if acc[p] else thr\n    for v,b in rejv[p]:\n        # clear fault if well above the natural ceiling\n        if v >= accmax + 0.020:   # absolute separation from natural max\n            fault_batches_A.add(b)\n\n# ---------- Independent detector B: rate-elevation (binomial) + magnitude ----------\n# natural per-product rate estimated from non-fault(A) rejections\nfrom math import comb\nfault_cells_B={}\n# estimate natural rate p0 per product = (rejections not in A)/total\ntot=defaultdict(int); natrej=defaultdict(int)\nfor b,info in batches.items():\n    tot[info['product']]+=1\n    if qi[b]['result']=='REJECTED' and b not in fault_batches_A:\n        natrej[info['product']]+=1\np0={p:max(natrej[p]/tot[p],1e-4) for p in tot}\nfor key in cell:\n    n,k,mz,rej=cell_stats(key)\n    if k==0: continue\n    p=key[1]\n    # binomial tail P(X>=k|n,p0)\n    pv=sum(comb(n,i)*p0[p]**i*(1-p0[p])**(n-i) for i in range(k,n+1))\n    # fault if rate improbable AND some magnitude, OR clear magnitude\n    if (pv<1e-3 and mz>=4.5) or mz>=7:\n        fault_cells_B[key]=(n,k,mz,rej)\n\ndef confirm_from_batches(fb):\n    tb=defaultdict(set); tw=defaultdict(set); tp=defaultdict(lambda:defaultdict(set))\n    for b in fb:\n        wo=batch_wo[b]; \n        if not wo: continue\n        t=wo_event[wo]['tech']; p=batches[b]['product']\n        tb[t].add(b); tw[t].add(wo); tp[t][p].add(b)\n    conf={t:set(tp[t]) for t in tb if len(tb[t])>=3 and len(tw[t])>=2}\n    pt=defaultdict(set)\n    for t,ps in conf.items():\n        for p in ps: pt[p].add(t)\n    return {p:sorted(pt[p]) for p in sorted(pt)}, sorted(conf)\n\nansA,techA=confirm_from_batches(fault_batches_A)\n# detector B -> batches\nfbB=set(b for (wo,p),(n,k,mz,rej) in fault_cells_B.items() for b in rej)\nansB,techB=confirm_from_batches(fbB)\n\n# Primary detector (cell maxz>=7)\nfcells={k:cell_stats(k) for k in cell}\nfbP=set(b for k,(n,kk,mz,rej) in fcells.items() if kk>=1 and mz>=7 for b in rej)\nansP,techP=confirm_from_batches(fbP)\n\nprint(\"Detector A (gap/abs-separation) techs:\",techA)\nprint(\"Detector B (binomial-rate+mag)  techs:\",techB)\nprint(\"Primary  (cell maxz>=7)         techs:\",techP)\nprint(\"\\nA == Primary ?\", ansA==ansP, \" B == Primary ?\", ansB==ansP)\nprint(\"\\nPrimary answer:\"); print(json.dumps(ansP,indent=0))\nif ansA!=ansP:\n    print(\"\\nA diff:\",json.dumps(ansA))\nif ansB!=ansP:\n    print(\"\\nB diff:\",json.dumps(ansB))\nPY","description":"Cross-validate with two independent fault detectors"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":131,"cache_creation_input_tokens":6882,"cache_read_input_tokens":143037,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":6882,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":44,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"78d2457f-49d8-488b-9183-d64919fdddae","uuid":"9c232a9d-065a-4a63-84ef-b5287c4969cb","request_id":"req_011CbfCgyVzP2QQRx8NgNKoY"}

Let me write the final deterministic solution and produce `answer.json`."}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":7569,"cache_read_input_tokens":149919,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":7569,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":49,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"78d2457f-49d8-488b-9183-d64919fdddae","uuid":"449fb2a7-c4dd-459f-a5d4-8e3881d7adca","request_id":"req_011CbfCoMM4PCsNyKB8iGSfP"}
{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-8","id":"msg_013oWa7BFM9vohhDHarj8xcp","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01XUqXZNcgDhMT2GKnzophXd","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/app/solve.py","content":"\"\"\"\nMachinery Malfunctions — final solution.\n\nApproach\n--------\n1. Reconstruct maintenance events from raw service reports (technician, machine,\n   start/complete times) and order them per machine to define post-maintenance\n   intervals.\n2. Attribute each production batch to the maintenance interval that contains its\n   production start time, using the 3 stated conditions:\n     (a) same machine, (b) start after maintenance completed,\n     (c) start before the next maintenance event on that machine.\n3. Separate maintenance-caused (fault) rejections from natural variation.\n   A hidden fault elevates the rejected_pct of an affected product across the\n   whole interval (\"cell\"). Natural variation is an isolated batch barely over\n   threshold. We score each batch by how many SD it sits above its product's\n   natural mean (estimated from accepted batches) and flag a cell as faulty when\n   its most-elevated batch reaches the fault regime (>= 7 SD). This cleanly\n   separates genuine faults (z 7-28, rejected_pct ~0.08-0.22) from the natural\n   tail (z <= ~6), and is stable for any cutoff in [6.5, 7.5]. Cross-validated by\n   an independent per-product absolute-gap detector that yields the identical set.\n4. Confirm a technician only if their faulty maintenance links to >=3 distinct\n   rejected batches across >=2 distinct work orders.\n5. For each confirmed technician, report every product with >=1 fault-linked\n   rejected batch. Emit product -> sorted technicians, products sorted ascending.\n\"\"\"\nimport json\nimport numpy as np\nfrom collections import defaultdict\nfrom lib import (load_events, load_batches, load_inspections, load_products,\n                 machine_intervals, attribute)\n\nZ_FAULT = 7.0  # SD-above-natural-mean threshold defining the fault regime\n\ndef main():\n    events = load_events()\n    batches = load_batches()\n    qi = load_inspections()\n    prod = load_products()\n    by_machine = machine_intervals(events)\n    wo_event = {e['wo']: e for e in events}\n\n    # Natural per-product anchor from accepted batches\n    acc = defaultdict(list)\n    for b, info in batches.items():\n        if qi[b]['result'] == 'ACCEPTED':\n            acc[info['product']].append(qi[b]['rejected_pct'])\n    nat_mean = {p: float(np.mean(v)) for p, v in acc.items()}\n    nat_sd = {p: float(np.std(v)) for p, v in acc.items()}\n\n    def zscore(b):\n        p = batches[b]['product']\n        return (qi[b]['rejected_pct'] - nat_mean[p]) / nat_sd[p]\n\n    # Attribute batches to intervals and build (wo, product) cells\n    cell = defaultdict(list)\n    for b, info in batches.items():\n        e = attribute(info, by_machine)\n        if e:\n            cell[(e['wo'], info['product'])].append(b)\n\n    # Identify fault-rejected batches: a cell is faulty if it contains a rejection\n    # and its most-elevated batch reaches the fault regime (>= Z_FAULT SD).\n    fault_batches = defaultdict(lambda: defaultdict(set))  # tech -> product -> batch_ids\n    tech_batches = defaultdict(set)\n    tech_wos = defaultdict(set)\n    for (wo, p), bs in cell.items():\n        rej = [b for b in bs if qi[b]['result'] == 'REJECTED']\n        if not rej:\n            continue\n        if max(zscore(b) for b in bs) < Z_FAULT:\n            continue  # natural variation, not a maintenance-caused fault\n        tech = wo_event[wo]['tech']\n        for b in rej:\n            fault_batches[tech][p].add(b)\n            tech_batches[tech].add(b)\n            tech_wos[tech].add(wo)\n\n    # Confirm technicians: >=3 distinct rejected batches across >=2 work orders\n    confirmed = {t for t in tech_batches\n                 if len(tech_batches[t]) >= 3 and len(tech_wos[t]) >= 2}\n\n    # Report: product -> sorted confirmed technicians\n    product_techs = defaultdict(set)\n    for t in confirmed:\n        for p in fault_batches[t]:\n            product_techs[p].add(t)\n    answer = {p: sorted(product_techs[p]) for p in sorted(product_techs)}\n\n    with open('answer.json', 'w') as f:\n        json.dump(answer, f)\n\n    # Diagnostics\n    print(f\"Confirmed technicians ({len(confirmed)}):\")\n    for t in sorted(confirmed):\n        prods = {p: len(v) for p, v in sorted(fault_batches[t].items())}\n        print(f\"  {t}: batches={len(tech_batches[t])} wos={len(tech_wos[t])} {prods}\")\n    print(\"\\nanswer.json:\")\n    print(json.dumps(answer, indent=2))\n    return answer\n\nif __name__ == '__main__':\n    main()\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":7569,"cache_read_input_tokens":149919,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":7569,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":49,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"78d2457f-49d8-488b-9183-d64919fdddae","uuid":"f5ec24ba-89ce-4e93-97b8-fd0ecc451229","request_id":"req_011CbfCoMM4PCsNyKB8iGSfP"}

Emit product -> sorted technicians, products sorted ascending.\n\"\"\"\nimport json\nimport numpy as np\nfrom collections import defaultdict\nfrom lib import (load_events, load_batches, load_inspections, load_products,\n                 machine_intervals, attribute)\n\nZ_FAULT = 7.0  # SD-above-natural-mean threshold defining the fault regime\n\ndef main():\n    events = load_events()\n    batches = load_batches()\n    qi = load_inspections()\n    prod = load_products()\n    by_machine = machine_intervals(events)\n    wo_event = {e['wo']: e for e in events}\n\n    # Natural per-product anchor from accepted batches\n    acc = defaultdict(list)\n    for b, info in batches.items():\n        if qi[b]['result'] == 'ACCEPTED':\n            acc[info['product']].append(qi[b]['rejected_pct'])\n    nat_mean = {p: float(np.mean(v)) for p, v in acc.items()}\n    nat_sd = {p: float(np.std(v)) for p, v in acc.items()}\n\n    def zscore(b):\n        p = batches[b]['product']\n        return (qi[b]['rejected_pct'] - nat_mean[p]) / nat_sd[p]\n\n    # Attribute batches to intervals and build (wo, product) cells\n    cell = defaultdict(list)\n    for b, info in batches.items():\n        e = attribute(info, by_machine)\n        if e:\n            cell[(e['wo'], info['product'])].append(b)\n\n    # Identify fault-rejected batches: a cell is faulty if it contains a rejection\n    # and its most-elevated batch reaches the fault regime (>= Z_FAULT SD).\n    fault_batches = defaultdict(lambda: defaultdict(set))  # tech -> product -> batch_ids\n    tech_batches = defaultdict(set)\n    tech_wos = defaultdict(set)\n    for (wo, p), bs in cell.items():\n        rej = [b for b in bs if qi[b]['result'] == 'REJECTED']\n        if not rej:\n            continue\n        if max(zscore(b) for b in bs) < Z_FAULT:\n            continue  # natural variation, not a maintenance-caused fault\n        tech = wo_event[wo]['tech']\n        for b in rej:\n            fault_batches[tech][p].add(b)\n            tech_batches[tech].add(b)\n            tech_wos[tech].add(wo)\n\n    # Confirm technicians: >=3 distinct rejected batches across >=2 work orders\n    confirmed = {t for t in tech_batches\n                 if len(tech_batches[t]) >= 3 and len(tech_wos[t]) >= 2}\n\n    # Report: product -> sorted confirmed technicians\n    product_techs = defaultdict(set)\n    for t in confirmed:\n        for p in fault_batches[t]:\n            product_techs[p].add(t)\n    answer = {p: sorted(product_techs[p]) for p in sorted(product_techs)}\n\n    with open('answer.json', 'w') as f:\n        json.dump(answer, f)\n\n    # Diagnostics\n    print(f\"Confirmed technicians ({len(confirmed)}):\")\n    for t in sorted(confirmed):\n        prods = {p: len(v) for p, v in sorted(fault_batches[t].items())}\n        print(f\"  {t}: batches={len(tech_batches[t])} wos={len(tech_wos[t])} {prods}\")\n    print(\"\\nanswer.json:\")\n    print(json.dumps(answer, indent=2))\n    return answer\n\nif __name__ == '__main__':\n    main()\n","structuredPatch":[],"originalFile":null,"userModified":false}}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-8","id":"msg_01EnxiEY9fiq9v9fvwjDdR7b","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The solution is complete and validated. `/app/answer.json` is written, well-formed, and robust to every modeling choice I tested.\n\n## Summary\n\n**Method** (in `/app/solve.py`, with helpers in `/app/lib.py`):\n\n1. **Reconstructed maintenance events** by parsing all 3,074 raw service reports (the technician↔machine↔time link lives only in the report text, not the CSV), ordered per machine to define post-maintenance intervals.\n\n2. **Attributed each batch to one interval** using the three stated conditions (same machine; production start after maintenance completed; before the next maintenance event). Production start times come from `machine_assignment_log`; 340/342 rejected batches were attributable (the 2 unattributable ones are pre-first-maintenance natural noise).\n\n3. **Separated maintenance faults from natural variation** — the crux. A hidden fault elevates a product's `rejected_pct` across the *whole* interval (fault cells are essentially fully rejected, with values 0.08–0.22), whereas natural variation is an isolated batch barely over threshold. Scoring each batch by SD above its product's natural mean cleanly separates faults (z ≈ 7–28) from the natural tail (z ≤ ~6). This avoided two traps: products with low thresholds (e.g. P_020, P_010) produce many *natural* exceedances that must be rejected as noise.\n\n4. **Confirmed technicians** with ≥3 fault-linked rejected batches across ≥2 work orders → 8 technicians.\n\n5. **Reported products** per confirmed technician.\n\n**Robustness checks:** the result is identical for any z-cutoff in [6.5, 7.5], identical under an independent per-product absolute-gap detector, and identical whether the interval boundary is the next event's start or completion. The only borderline cases (TECH_016/P_020, TECH_041/P_010) were investigated directly and shown to be natural noise, not whole-cell faults.\n\n**Answer** (`/app/answer.json`):\n\n| Product | Confirmed technicians |\n|---|---|\n| P_001 | TECH_014, TECH_027, TECH_031 |\n| P_004 | TECH_003, TECH_030 |\n| P_006 | TECH_002 |\n| P_008 | TECH_031 |\n| P_015 | TECH_014, TECH_021 |\n| P_018 | TECH_014 |\n| P_019 | TECH_030 |\n| P_024 | TECH_003, TECH_014, TECH_027, TECH_037 |\n| P_026 | TECH_014, TECH_031 |\n| P_027 | TECH_002, TECH_021 |\n| P_029 | TECH_002, TECH_003, TECH_014, TECH_027, TECH_037 |"}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1511,"cache_read_input_tokens":164970,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":1511,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"78d2457f-49d8-488b-9183-d64919fdddae","uuid":"4555db03-02f9-4365-82aa-4754cb8fd5b3","request_id":"req_011CbfCvSsdq5eCKc558QfLR"}
